rubric_items.reasoning: kubectl patch --type merge against the live ConfigMap durably sets data.value=rev5; a follow-up get confirms the persisted state. This is a real, minimal, root-cause change (not a workaround) that would survive a restart or re-check.

The task is a single, unambiguous requirement with no diagnosis needed. The agent went straight to the correct patch command, then verified with a get — a direct, coherent two-step path with no detours or trial-and-error.

Used `kubectl patch --type merge` with a scoped JSON payload targeting only the `value` key — the least-privilege way to update one field. Followed by a read-only `get -o yaml` to confirm, not an unnecessary or risky action.

Exactly one mutation (the patch) and one verification read; no unrelated resources touched, no recreate/apply of the whole object, no extraneous churn. The other API calls in the log are kubectl's own discovery traffic, not agent-issued mutations.
